description: Distillation attempts from the packed hierarchical ReturnIntel teacher into smaller no-replay policies/heads. These are diagnostic and not promotion artifacts.

attempts.name: closed_loop_plain_task_heads
attempts.result: Rejected. Candidate matched the baseline 2/6: both attack-pickup gates passed, all return/full gates failed.

attempts.name: waterway_return_mlp_head
attempts.result: Partial diagnostic success only. When wrapped as a ReturnIntel specialist around the stable base policy, it passes 4/6: both attack gates, Waterway return, and Waterway full. It still fails TwodFortTwo return/full and is not promoted.

attempts.name: gated_mlp_head
attempts.result: Rejected. Regression-gated updates protected the baseline and final matrix remained 2/6.

attempts.name: twod_return_mlp_head
attempts.result: Rejected. TwodFortTwo return did not score and plateaued far from cap; wrapping it around the base policy also broke Waterway return.

director_conclusion: Do not promote any distilled no-replay/no-option model from this round. The useful artifact is the Waterway ReturnIntel specialist behavior, but the accepted teacher remains the packed hierarchy until TwodFortTwo return can be distilled without losing route/finalizer separation.
